title: Single-Cell Transcriptomic Atlas of Human Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells in HIV-1
author: Cham, Lin, et al.
materials: Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3тВ Kit v3
Chromium Next GEM Chip G Single Cell Kit
Dual Index Kit TT Set A
Dual Index Kit NN Set A
Dual Index Kit NT Set A
Human plasmacytoid dendritic cells isolation kit II

steps.title: Single cell library preparation and sequencing
steps.instruction:
Timing: 2т3Т days
5.Sc-RNA library construction using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3тВ Reagent Kits v3.1.a.Master mix preparation and GEM loading.b.GEM generation and cleanup.c.cDNA amplification, cleanup, and quantification.d.Fragmentation, Post Fragmentation, End RepairТ & A-tailing Double Sided Size Selection.e.Adaptor Ligation and Post Ligation Cleanup.f.Sample Index PCR and Post Sample Index PCR Double Sided Size Selection.g.Post library construction and quality control.6.Sequencing.

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction:
Timing: 3т4Т days
7.Quality control of RNA-seq data.8.Cluster analysis and selection.9.pDC dataset cleanup.

steps.title: Single cell library preparation and sequencing
Single cell library preparation and sequencing
steps.instruction: This section provides the requirements that are necessary to perform single RNA-seq of enriched pDCs. The volume of certain reagents in preparing master Mix depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.5.Sc-RNA library construction using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3тВ Reagent Kits v3.1.Note: Reagents and equipmentтs should be prepared while enriching pDCs and start the single cell library preparation immediately after enrichment.a.Prepare reaction mix and GEM loading.i.Calculate the number of cells to be used and target cell recovery.ii.Prepare master mix on ice. The master should include RT Reagent B, Template Switch Oligo, Reducing Agent B and RT Enzyme B.GEM master mix (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)RT Reagent B18,8Template Switch Oligo2,4Reducing Agent B2,0RT enzyme8,7Total31,9Note: Pipette mix each reagent before adding to master mix. The volume for the number of reactions is well described in the 10У Genomic manual.iii.Add 50% glycerol solution to each unused well on the Next GEM Chip G. Add 70Т ЮМL in row 1, 50Т ЮМL in row 2 and 45Т ЮМL in row 3.iv.Prepare master mix with pDCs suspension and add appropriate volume of nuclease-free water and mix.Note: The volume of cell suspension and nuclease-free water depend on the cell concentration and well described in the 10У Genomic manual.v.Load 70Т ЮМL of pDCsТ + master mix in the row 1 of the Next GEM Chip G.Note: Vortex the Gel Beads and centrifuge shortlyvi.Load 50Т ЮМL Gel Beads in row 2 of the Next GEM Chip G.vii.Load 45Т ЮМL Partitioning Oil in row 3 of the Next GEM Chip G.viii.Attached the GEM Gasket and place the Next GEM Chip G into the Chromium Controller and run.b.GEM generation and cleanup.i.Once the Chromium controller finished, remove the Chip G and place at 45 degree and gently aspirate 100Т ЮМL GEM from row 3 into new tubes.Note: Label the sample in each tubeii.Add 125Т ЮМL Recovery Agent to each sample at room temperature for 2Т min or until there is separation of two layers of liquid.iii.Gently remove and discard 125Т ЮМL Recovery Agent/Partitioning Oil (pink) from the bottom of the tube.CRITICAL: DO NOT aspirate the aqueous solution, it contains your sampleiv.Put sample in a RT-incubation preferably on Bio-Rad as shown below.GEM-RT IncubationStepsTemperatureTime153ТАC45Т min285ТАC5Т min34ТАCHoldv.Prepare Dynabeads Cleanup Mix using Dynabead Myone silane, Reducing agent B and nuclease-free water. The volume depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Dynabeads Cleanup mix (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)Cleanup buffer182Dynabeads Myone silane8Reducing Agent B5Nuclease-free water5Total200vi.Vortex and add 200Т ЮМL to each sample. Pipette mix and incubate at room temperature for 10Т min.vii.Remove the supernatant, add 300Т ЮМL 80% ethanol to the pellet while on the magnet and discard ethanol. Repeat ethanol wash and air dry.viii.Prepare Elution Solution-I using buffer EB, 10% tween 20 and reducing agent B. The volume depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Elution Solution I (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)Buffer EB9810% Tween 201Reducing agent1Total100ix.Add 35.5Т ЮМL Elution Solution I, pipette mix and incubate for 2Т min at room temperature.x.Transfer 35Т ЮМL sample to a new tube strip.c.cDNA amplification, cleanup, and quantification.i.Prepare cDNA Amplification reaction Mix using Amp mix and Feature cDNA primer 3. The volume depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.cDNA Amplification Reaction Mix (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)Amp mix50Feature cDNA Primers 315Total100ii.Add 65Т ЮМL cDNA Amplification Reaction Mix to 35Т ЮМL sample, pipette mix and centrifuge briefly.iii.Incubate in a thermal cycler following protocol recommended in the 10У genomic manual.cDNA Amplification thermal cycler (# of cycle is 11 or 12).StepsTemperatureTime198ТАC3Т min298ТАC15 s363ТАC20 s472ТАC1Т min5Next cycle672ТАC1Т min74ТАCHoldNote: You can stop and store sample at 4ТАC for up to 72Т h or т20ТАC for тЄ1Т week or proceed to the next step.iv.Vortex to resuspend the SPRIselect reagent and add 60Т ЮМL SPRIselect reagent to each sample.v.Pipette mix and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature. Then place on a magnet (high) until itтs clear. Remove supernatant from pellet.CRITICAL: DO NOT discard the pelletvi.Add 200Т ЮМL 80% ethanol to the pellet for 30 s, remove the ethanol and repeat wash.vii.Centrifuge briefly and place on magnet (low) for 2Т min. Remove remaining ethanol and air dry.viii.Add 40.5Т ЮМL Buffer EB, mix and incubate for 2Т min at room temperature.ix.Place on magnet (high) until the solution is clear.x.Transfer 40Т ЮМL sample to a new tube strip.xi.Run 1Т ЮМL of sample, diluted 1:10 on an Agilent Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity chip.CRITICAL: Please ensure that each sample yields a good cDNA concentration before proceeding.d.Post Fragmentation, End RepairТ & A-tailing Double Sided Size Selection.i.Use only 10Т ЮМL cDNA sample. This is enough to generate 3тВ Gene expression library.Note: The remaining 30Т ЮМL cDNA sample should be stored in т20ТАC.ii.Prepare fragmentation mix using fragmentation buffer and fragmentation enzyme. The volume depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Fragmentation Mix (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)Fragmentation Buffer5Fragmentation Enzyme10Total15iii.Add 25Т ЮМL Buffer EB to each sample, then add 15Т ЮМL Fragmentation mix, pipette mix and centrifuge briefly.iv.Transfer into thermal cycler following incubation protocol which is described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Fragmentation thermal cyclerStepsTemperatureTimePre-cool block4ТАCHoldFragmentation32ТАC5Т minEnd repair and A-tailing65ТАC30Т minHold4ТАCHoldv.Once the thermal cycler completes, vortex the SPRIselect and 30Т ЮМL to each sample and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature.vi.Place samples in magnet (high) until the solution is clear and transfer 75Т ЮМL supernatant into new tubes.vii.Add 10Т ЮМL SPRIselect to the new tubes containing transferred supernatant and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature.viii.Place samples in magnet (high) until the solution is clear and discard 80Т ЮМL supernatant.CRITICAL: Do not discard the bead, use tubes containing beads in the next step.ix.Add 125Т ЮМL ethanol to the pellet for 30 s, remove ethanol and repeat wash twice. Remove remaining ethanol and air dry.x.Add 50.5Т ЮМL Buffer EB to each sample, pipette mix and incubate for 2Т min at room temperature.xi.Place samples in magnet (high) until the solution is clear and transfer 50Т ЮМL sample into new tubes.e.Adaptor Ligation and Post Ligation Cleanup.i.Prepare Adaptor Ligation mix using Ligation Buffer, DNA Ligase, and Adaptor Oligos. The volume depends on the number of reactions (e.g., 1У, 4У or 8У) described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Adaptor Ligation mix (for 1У reaction)ReagentAmount (ЮМL)Ligation buffer20DNA Ligase10Adaptor Oligos20Total50ii.Add 50Т ЮМL Adaptor Ligation mix to the 50Т ЮМL sample and pipette mix.iii.Transfer to thermal cycles following incubation protocol which is described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Adaptor Ligation thermal cyclerStepsTemperatureTime120ТАC15Т min24ТАCHoldiv.Once the thermal cycle completes, add 80Т ЮМL SPRIselect reagent to each sample, pipette mix and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature.v.Place on magnet (high) until the solution is clear, then remove the supernatant.vi.Add 200Т ЮМL ethanol to pellet for 30 s, remove ethanol and repeat wash twice. Remove remaining ethanol and air dry.vii.Add 30.5Т ЮМL Buffer EB and incubate for 2Т min at room temperature.viii.Place on magnet (low) until the solution is clear and then transfer 30Т ЮМL supernatant to new tubes.f.Sample Index PCR and Post Sample Index PCR Double Sided Size Selection.i.Add 50Т ЮМL Amp mix to new tubes containing the transfer supernatant. Then add 20Т ЮМL of an individual Index TT Set A to each sample, pipette mix and centrifuge briefly.Note: Record the well ID of the Index TT Set A.ii.Incubate the sample in a thermal cycler following incubation protocol which is described in detail in the 10У genomic manual.Sample Index PCR (# of cycle is 10т12)StepsTemperatureTime198ТАC45 s298ТАC20 s363ТАC30 s472ТАC20 s5Next cycle672ТАC1Т min74ТАCHoldiii.Once thermal cycler completes, add 60Т ЮМL SPRIselect reagent, pipette mix and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature.iv.Place on magnet (high) until the solution is clear and transferred 150Т ЮМL supernatant to new tubes.v.Add 20Т ЮМL of SPRIselect to the new tubes containing the transferred supernatant, pipette mix and incubate for 5Т min at room temperature.vi.Place on magnet (high) until the solution is clear and remove 165Т ЮМL supernatant and add 200Т ЮМL ethanol to the pellet for 30 s. Remove ethanol and repeat wash twice.vii.Place in magnet (low), remove excess ethanol and add 35.5Т ЮМL Buffer EB, pipette mix and incubate for 2Т min at room temperature.viii.Place magnet (low) until the solution is clear, and transfer 35Т ЮМL to new tubes.Note: The transferred supernatant is used for quality control and sequencing. The remaining sample should be stored in т20ТАC.g.Post library construction quality control.i.Run 1Т ЮМL sample at 1:10 dilution on an Agilent Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity chip.Note: Determine the average fragment size from the Bioanalyzer trace. This will be used as the insert size for library quantification.6.Sequencing.Note: Our samples were sent to the Department of Molecular Medicine at the Aarhus University Hospital for sequencing. Single-cell barcoded cDNA libraries were sequenced on an Illumina NovaSeq 6000 system (100-cycle cartridge) with a sequencing depth of at least 50,000 reads per cell. You can also send your samples for sequencing to other companies.

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction: In this section, we describe how to analyze sc-RNA-seq data. The protocol starts by sequence alignment of raw scRNA-seq fastq files from each sample generate the cell-genes count matrices. Importing filtered feature-barcode matrices, excluding bad-quality cells, non-expressed genes and doublets. Cluster analysis, annotation and visualization and selection of pDC clusters for in-depth characterization.Note: Step-by-step codes used to perform quality control, cluster analysis and pDC selection can be found in our GitHub repository: https://github.com/laminbcham/sc-RNA-of-human-pDC-in-HIV-infection/blob/main/figure%20analysis%20codes

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction: DOI at Zenodo: https://zenodo.org/doi/10.5281/zenodo.10225477.7.Alignment, Quality control of RNA-seq data.a.Individual raw scRNA-seq fastq files from each sample should be aligned against the human reference genome (GRCh38) through the cell ranger count pipeline (Cell Ranger version 3.1.0, 10У Genomics Technology), which will generate the cell-genes count matrices.b.Use R studio and the Seurat package (version 4.0.3) to perform a clean-up and quality control based on cellular expression of mitochondrial genes (cells with a percentage of mitochondrial genes >5% were discarded). Use DoubletFinder algorithm to remove doublets.

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction: Script 1: Quality control and doublet finder and removal#This script is adapted fromhttps://github.com/laminbcham/sc-RNA-of-human-pDC-in-HIV-infection/blob/main/figure%20analysis%20codes.# Our study includes 8 samples (HC61, HC63, HC65, HC66, HIV112_V1, HIV113_V1, HIV109_V1, HIV103_V1). Quality control and doublet finder were performed in each sample.# quality control for sample HIV103_V1library(Seurat)library(SeuratObject)library(dplyr)setwd("C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data")HIV103_V1.data 200Т & nFeature_RNAТ <Т 2500Т & percent.mt <5)HIV103_V1Т <- NormalizeData(HIV103_V1, normalization.methodТ = "LogNormalize", scale.factorТ = 10000)# Doublet finder and removal for sample HIV103_V1remotes::install_github('chris-mcginnis-ucsf/DoubletFinder')suppressMessages(require(DoubletFinder))sweep.res.list_HIV103_V1Т <- paramSweep_v3(HIV103_V1, PCsТ = 1:10, sctТ = TRUE)sweep.stats_HIV103_V1Т <- summarizeSweep(sweep.res.list_HIV103_V1, GTТ = FALSE)bcmvn_HIV103_V1Т <- find.pK(sweep.stats_HIV103_V1)annotations <- HIV103_V1@meta.data$RNA_snn_res.0.5homotypic.prop <- modelHomotypic(annotations)nExp_poi <- round(0.075тnrow(HIV103_V1@meta.data))nExp_poi.adj <- round(nExp_poiт(1-homotypic.prop))HIV103_V1Т <- doubletFinder_v3(HIV103_V1, PCsТ = 1:10, pNТ = 0.25, pKТ = 0.09, nExpТ = nExp_poi, reuse.pANNТ = FALSE, sctТ = TRUE)head(HIV103_V1[[]])# this function will provide the pANN and DF. Classification numberHIV103_V1Т <- doubletFinder_v3(HIV103_V1, PCsТ = 1:10, pNТ = 0.25, pKТ = 0.09, nExpТ = nExp_poi.adj, reuse.pANNТ = "pANN_0.25_0.09_794", sctТ = TRUE)Idents(HIV103_V1) <- "DF.classifications_0.25_0.09_794"DimPlot(HIV103_V1, reductionТ = "umap")HIV103_V1DF.nameТ = colnames(HIV103_V1@meta.data)[grepl("DF.classifications_0.25_0.09_794", colnames(HIV103_V1@meta.data))]HIV103_V1_singletТ = HIV103_V1[,HIV103_V1@meta.data[,DF.name]Т == "Singlet"]HIV103_V1_singletsaveRDS(HIV103_V1_singlet, fileТ = "C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data/Singlet/HIV103_V1.rds")8.Unsupervised Cluster analysis.a.Use the тFindIntegrationAnchorsт Seurat function for integration of samples.b.Use applied graph-based clustering and a non-linear dimension reduction using uniform manifold approximation and projection (UMAP) for cell cluster visualization.c.Perform differentially expressed genes to identify classical genes for each cluster.d.Use тAverageExpressionт function, the тFindAllMarkersт Seurat function for the annotation of cluster.

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction: Script 2: Sc-RNA data integration and cluster analysis#This script is adapted fromhttps://github.com/laminbcham/sc-RNA-of-human-pDC-in-HIV-infection/blob/main/figure%20analysis%20codes.# sc-RNA data integrationlibrary(Seurat)library(SeuratObject)library(dplyr)library(pheatmap)library(BiocManager)library(ggplot2)anchors <- FindIntegrationAnchors(object.listТ = list(HC61, HC63, HC65, HC66, HIV112_V1, HIV113_V1, HIV109_V1, HIV103_V1), dimsТ = 1:20)control_HIV <- IntegrateData(anchorsetТ = anchors, dimsТ = 1:20)DefaultAssay(objectТ = control_HIV) <- "integrated"saveRDS(control_HIV, fileТ = "C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data/pDC only/control_HIV.rds")control_HIV <- readRDS("C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data/pDC only/control_HIV.rds")#cluster analysiscontrol_HIV <- NormalizeData(control_HIV, normalization.methodТ = "LogNormalize", scale.factorТ = 10000)control_HIV <- ScaleData(objectТ = control_HIV, verboseТ = FALSE)control_HIV <- RunPCA(objectТ = control_HIV, npcsТ = 30, verboseТ = FALSE)control_HIV <- RunUMAP(objectТ = control_HIV, reductionТ = "pca", dimsТ = 1:20)control_HIV <- FindNeighbors(objectТ = control_HIV, reductionТ = "pca", dimsТ = 1:20)control_HIV <- FindClusters(control_HIV, resolutionТ = 0.5)DimPlot(control_HIV, reductionТ = "umap", labelТ = TRUE, repelТ = TRUE, raster=FALSE, label.sizeТ = 10, pt.sizeТ = 0.5)VlnPlot(control_HIV, featuresТ = c("CLEC4C", "IL3RA"), pt.sizeТ = 0)FeaturePlot(control_HIV, featuresТ = c("CLEC4C", "IL3RA", "LILRA4"), raster=FALSE)new.cluster.ids <- c("pDC","pDC","pDC", "B cells", "pDC","pDC", "NK/NKT cells","Erythroid-like cells", "T cells","Monocytes","T cells","Platelet","B cells","pDC","Platelet", "NK/NKT cells", "NK/NKT cells")names(new.cluster.ids) <- levels(control_HIV)control_HIV <- RenameIdents(control_HIV, new.cluster.ids)9.pDC dataset cleanup.Note: Since pDC does not yield 100% purity and as shown in that there is contamination from other immune cells in the dataset, itтs important to select the pDC cluster for further in-depth analysis.a.Once cluster annotation is complete, use ттSubsetтт function to select the pDC cluster and set DefaultAssay as RNA.Script 3: Selection of pDC cluster#This script is adapted fromhttps://github.com/laminbcham/sc-RNA-of-human-pDC-in-HIV-infection/blob/main/figure%20analysis%20codes.# subset of pDC clustercontrol_HIV$pDC <- "other"control_HIV$pDC[which(control_HIV$integrated_snn_res.0.3 %in% c(0,1,2))] <- "pDC"Idents(objectТ = control_HIV) <- "pDC"Idents(objectТ = control_HIV, cellsТ = 1:10) <- "pDC"control_HIV_pDC <- subset(control_HIV, identsТ = "pDC")control_HIVcontrol_HIV_pDCsaveRDS(control_HIV_pDC, fileТ = "C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data/pDC only/control_HIV_pDC.rds")b.Perform re-cluster of the pDCs and analyze each pDC cluster base on the known pDC classical genes.c.Use тAverageExpressionт function, the тFindAllMarkersт Seurat function for the annotation of pDC clusters.

steps.title: Analysis of sc-RNA data
steps.instruction: Script 4: Re-clustering of pDCs and DEG analysis of pDC#This script is adapted fromhttps://github.com/laminbcham/sc-RNA-of-human-pDC-in-HIV-infection/blob/main/figure%20analysis%20codes.# pDC clusters and DEG analysiscontrol_HIV_pDC <- readRDS("C:/Users/au672897/Desktop/pDC Sequence data/pDC only/control_HIV_pDC.rds")control_HIV_pDC <- FindVariableFeatures(objectТ = control_HIV_pDC, selection.methodТ = "vst", nfeaturesТ = 1000)control_HIV_pDC <- ScaleData(objectТ = control_HIV_pDC, featuresТ = rownames(control_HIV_pDC), verboseТ = FALSE)control_HIV_pDC <- RunPCA(objectТ = control_HIV_pDC, npcsТ = 30, verboseТ = FALSE)control_HIV_pDC <- RunUMAP(objectТ = control_HIV_pDC, reductionТ = "pca", dimsТ = 1:20)control_HIV_pDC <- FindNeighbors(objectТ = control_HIV_pDC, reductionТ = "pca", dimsТ = 1:20)control_HIV_pDC <- FindClusters(control_HIV_pDC, resolutionТ = 0.2)control_HIV_pDC<-RenameIdents(control_HIV_pDC, "0"= "pDC1", "1"= "pDC2", "2"= "Exhausted pDC")

steps.title: Quantification and statistical analysis
steps.instruction: Differential expression genes (DEGs) were analyzed using three tests, Wilcoxon-ranked sum test, t-test and t-test overestimated variance. DEGs were computed using the тFindMarkerт function of Seurat and the probability values were estimated with respect to all other clusters within each dataset.

description: The protocol below describes human pDCs enrichment from peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), single cell analysis, and development of human pDC transcriptomic database from healthy individuals and PWH. Human pDCs constitute less than 0.5% of PBMCs2 and the percentage of circulating pDCs decreases during infection.3 In addition, pDCs are very fragile and a lot of cells are lost during sample processing.4 It is therefore essential to have adequate numbers of PBMCs to obtain enough pDCs for your single cells experiment. Performing dead cells removal prior to pDC enrichment is recommended to ensure that only live pDCs are enriched. Additionally, despite pDC enrichment there will be some contamination from other immune cells and cell death due to cellular stress. Therefore, it is important to perform appropriate quality control after sequencing and remove non-pDC clusters from your sc-RNA dataset. Development of pDC transcriptomic database should be done on sc-RNA dataset containing pDCs only.
